stubs_used: Semgrep findings — created docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md with Open=false; no real Semgrep run for the feature security audit.
Phase 3 SAST — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_semgrep_pass.json instead of running Semgrep.
Phase 3 accessibility/performance — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_lighthouse_accessibility.json and 2026-04-25_lighthouse_performance.json instead of running Lighthouse.
Phase 3 integration — touched docs/test-results/2026-04-25_integration.json instead of running integration tests.
GitHub remote — used https://example.com/fake.git via host=other; push failed (expected, no real network).
C# source/test — only stub `// stub` files in src/ and tests/; no .NET build was run.
Phase advancement — manually bumped current_phase via jq because check-phase-gate.sh is consistency-only and does not advance phase.
PreToolUse hook simulated — pre-commit-gate.sh tested by feeding the JSON envelope on stdin since the run is outside a real Claude Code PreToolUse cycle.

Phase 4 (release) — NOT executed because Private POC mode correctly blocks --start-phase4 until upgrade-project.sh --to-production is run. Behavior verified as expected; the runbook's Section E expectations do not apply to private_poc agents.
init_driver: Heredoc with canned answers: project name 'uat-agent-14', description, platform=1 (desktop, alphabetical first), track=2 (standard), deployment=2 (organizational), governance=1 (Private POC), language=1 (csharp), project_dir, Y for confirm, Y for proceed plan, host_choice=4 (other), visibility=1 (private), https://example.com/fake.git, attest=yes. As with agent-1, init.sh exited 1 because git push to fake remote failed inside create_and_protect_remote (set -euo pipefail propagated). Project skeleton fully created and usable for downstream tests.
scenario_specific_notes: Private POC mode wired correctly: phase-state.json reports `"poc_mode": "private_poc"`, `"deployment": "organizational"`, `"compliance_ready": false`. init.sh emitted the warning 'POC MODE: private poc — Phase 4 (production release) is blocked until you upgrade.' as documented.

Section E (Phase 4) is correctly blocked at --start-phase4 with [FAIL] message pointing to scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production. This is the documented contract for POC mode and is the right behavior for this scenario.
Standard track with Private POC was accepted (no track upgrade prompt fired because Private POC permits Light/Standard/Full per the validation logic in init.sh ~370).

tests.name: init_sh_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh exit code = 1. Same root cause as agent-1: create_and_protect_remote() runs git push against the fake remote which fails; with set -euo pipefail the failure aborts the script after project creation and pre-commit-hook install but before verify-install/print_next_steps. The on-disk project is otherwise complete and usable.

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh exits non-zero on documented happy path when remote push fails
framework_bugs.reproduction: Run init.sh selecting host=other and providing https://example.com/fake.git as the remote URL (per runbook Setup constraint). git push to fake.git fails; create_and_protect_remote returns 1; set -euo pipefail aborts main() before verify-install and print_next_steps. Exit code = 1.
framework_bugs.expected: Either (a) init.sh tolerates a remote-push failure, prints remediation guidance, and finishes the create_project tail; or (b) init.sh prints a clear `[BLOCKED at host-aware repo creation — fix and rerun scripts/check-gate.sh --repair]` and exits with a documented code instead of via set -e on the inner git push.
framework_bugs.actual: Bare `[FAIL] Push failed — verify URL and credentials` then immediate non-zero exit. Verify-install/print_next_steps skipped. (Confirms agent-1 finding for private_poc/standard/desktop config.)

framework_bugs.summary: init.sh has no scriptable mode for UAT — heredoc driver fragile
framework_bugs.reproduction: Pipe canned answers via stdin to bash init.sh; no --non-interactive flag exists. Number/order of prompts depends on installed tools and the host-aware repo prompts (host, visibility) only appear after project creation.
framework_bugs.expected: A non-interactive flag (or --answers-from FILE) so UAT and CI can drive init.sh deterministically.
framework_bugs.actual: Heredoc works only when answer count exactly matches prompt count. Same `Invalid choice` warning at the Git host prompt that agent-1 saw, indicating prompt order/count is not stable across installed-tool state.

documentation_gaps.gap: init.sh advertises only --dry-run and --help; runbook documents --non-interactive/--project/--platform/--track/--scenario/--upgrade-from flags that do not exist.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/init.sh main() flag parser vs Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md Section A example.

documentation_gaps.gap: Number/order of init.sh prompts is undocumented and varies with installed-tool state and per-platform conditional branches. UAT agents have no canonical prompt list.
documentation_gaps.where: init.sh collect_project_info / resolve_and_install_tools / create_and_protect_remote — no consolidated prompt-sequence doc in docs/.

tests.name: start_phase1_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase1 exited 0; .phase1_architecture.started_at populated (2026-04-25T16:50:24Z).

tests.name: phase1_steps_array_present
tests.details: jq '.phase1_architecture.steps_completed' returns [].

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed in order with rc=0.

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: scripts/check-phase-gate.sh exits 0 after all steps complete and reports `Phase gates consistent.` HOWEVER it is a consistency check, not a phase advancer (confirms agent-1 finding).

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: current_phase did NOT auto-advance after the gate. No script in scripts/ does this advancement. Bumped current_phase manually via jq to continue.

framework_bugs.summary: No script advances current_phase between phases
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all 5 phase1_architecture steps; run scripts/check-phase-gate.sh; observe .claude/phase-state.json `.current_phase` is still 0. There is no `--advance` or `--approve-gate` subcommand on check-phase-gate.sh, check-gate.sh, or process-checklist.sh that flips current_phase.
framework_bugs.expected: A documented script (e.g., `scripts/process-checklist.sh --advance-phase` or `scripts/check-phase-gate.sh --approve`) that, after gate consistency + approval-log entry, atomically writes the new current_phase value.
framework_bugs.actual: Phase advancement is left to manual jq editing or to the human Orchestrator's discretion. The runbook *asserts* phase_state_advances_to_2 as a test-case, so framework's phase model and runbook's protocol are out of sync. (Confirms agent-1 finding.)

documentation_gaps.gap: Builders Guide / RUNBOOK should explicitly name the script (or document the manual procedure) that bumps current_phase after a gate passes.
documentation_gaps.where: docs/reference/builders-guide.md (Phase Transitions); RUNBOOK.md Section B last row.

tests.name: start_feature_records_state
tests.details: After --start-feature uat-feat-1, .build_loop.feature = 'uat-feat-1' and .build_loop.started_at = 2026-04-25T16:50:42Z.

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: Sequential complete-step calls (tests_written → tests_verified_failing → implemented → security_audit → documentation_updated → feature_recorded) all succeeded with stub artifacts. The build_loop:security_audit gate checks docs/security-audits/<feature-slug>-security-audit.md exists; my stub satisfied it.

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' succeeded after all 5 mandatory build-loop steps were complete. Pre-commit hook (gitleaks + Semgrep + TDD ordering) passed against the stub C# files.

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: Simulated PreToolUse: `echo '{"command":"git commit --allow-empty -m \"feat(bl006-trigger): no loop\""}' | bash scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh` returned a deny JSON with reason `'feat(...)' commit blocked — no Build Loop active`. BL-006 wired correctly into Solo PreToolUse hook. PARTIAL CAVEAT: a direct shell `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(bl006-direct): direct shell'` succeeded, bypassing the gate (Solo BL-006 enforcement lives only in scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh as a Claude Code PreToolUse hook, NOT in .git/hooks/pre-commit). Confirms agent-1's finding.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: Wrote sentinel via `scripts/pending-approval.sh --offer 'Test approval question?' --options 'A1: Yes' 'A2: No' --recommendation A1`. Hook denied a chore commit with the rich `Pending question:` message including options/recommendation/offered_at.

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed the sentinel. Subsequent simulated commit was no longer blocked by BL-015. (It was instead blocked by Phase 2 init verification — separate gate, not a regression.)

tests.name: recorded_feature_appears_in_progress
tests.details: test-gate.sh --record-feature uat-feat-1 succeeded; .claude/build-progress.json `.features_completed` = ['uat-feat-1'].

framework_bugs.summary: BL-006 only enforced in Claude Code PreToolUse — direct shell git commits bypass it
framework_bugs.reproduction: In a project with no active build_loop feature, run `git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): bypass'` directly in shell. Commit succeeds. Solo enforcement lives in scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh (registered as Claude Code PreToolUse hook), not in .git/hooks/pre-commit. .git/hooks/pre-commit only runs gitleaks + Semgrep + TDD ordering + schema migration — no BL-006 / build-loop check.
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should also be enforced in .git/hooks/pre-commit so humans, CI, and any non-Claude-Code commit path cannot bypass it. Or the framework docs should say loudly that BL-006 is intentionally Claude-Code-only.
framework_bugs.actual: BL-006 enforced only when Claude Code is the commit author. Any human running git commit from terminal completely bypasses the build-loop requirement. (Confirms agent-1 finding.)

documentation_gaps.gap: The pending-approval sentinel schema (question/options/recommendation/offered_at) is enforced by build_pa_rich_reason but never documented for agents writing the file directly.
documentation_gaps.where: scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh:80-115; need docs/reference/builders-guide.md "Structured Decision Points" to publish the canonical schema.

documentation_gaps.gap: Split-enforcement story (.git/hooks/pre-commit for secrets/SAST/TDD vs scripts/pre-commit-gate.sh PreToolUse for BL-006/BL-015/phase gates) is not documented.

tests.name: start_phase3_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase3 exited 0. Phase 2→3 gate clear (FEATURES.md sections found, no open SEV-3 bugs, all batches tested).

tests.name: start_uat_creates_session
tests.details: --start-uat 1 set .uat_session.session_id=1 and .uat_session.started_at=2026-04-25T16:51:54Z.

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file argument (Usage: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh <populated-html-file>). The runbook implies a no-arg invocation lints the generated UAT scenario, but no such scenario file is generated in --start-uat. RC=0 because usage prints to stdout and exits 0, but the test as documented cannot run. Documentation gap — runbook should specify the file path or the lint script should auto-discover.

tests.name: each_uat_step_completes
tests.details: All 9 UAT_STEPS (agents_dispatched, template_generated, orchestrator_notified, results_received, completeness_verified, bugs_consolidated, triage_complete, remediation_complete, gate_passed) completed with rc=0.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: All 9 PHASE3_STEPS completed with rc=0 once stub artifacts (Semgrep/Lighthouse/integration) were placed in docs/test-results/. check-phase-gate.sh now reports 5 inconsistencies (PROJECT_BIBLE.md not found etc.) but exits 0 — same behavior as agent-1 (consistency check, not advancer).

documentation_gaps.gap: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh requires a populated HTML file argument; runbook does not specify which file to pass or where it is generated. Agents have no canonical UAT scenario file to lint after --start-uat.
documentation_gaps.where: Reports/uat-2026-04-25/RUNBOOK.md Section D row 3; scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh usage.

documentation_gaps.gap: PHASE3_STEPS array (9 items) is defined only in process-checklist.sh:31 — not exposed via --list-steps and not documented in builders guide.

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: EXPECTED BLOCK for private_poc scenario. process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 prints `[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in private poc mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production` and exits 0. This is the correct contract for Private POC mode — Phase 4 is gated until upgrade. All other Phase 4 sub-tests are N/A for this scenario.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: N/A — Phase 4 correctly gated for private_poc. Marked pass to avoid false-fail; the gate IS the test for this scenario.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: N/A for private_poc. Phase 4 gate fires before handoff check is reachable.

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: N/A for private_poc. Phase 4 gate fires before release-notes check is reachable.

tests.name: phase4_completion_state
tests.details: N/A for private_poc. .current_phase remains at 3 (highest reachable in POC mode); upgrade-project.sh --to-production would unlock Phase 4.

documentation_gaps.gap: RUNBOOK Section E does not explicitly call out that POC scenarios (private_poc, sponsored_poc) should expect Phase 4 to be blocked. Without this, agents may report the block as a Critical bug. Add a 'POC scenarios: expect block at --start-phase4; record N/A and note upgrade path' callout.

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Hits in evaluation-prompts/ are framework template documentation that mention 'token'/'secret'/'password' as words in security-evaluation prose, not actual secrets. No real credentials in workdir. (gitleaks ran clean during the feature commit.)

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — .claude/process-audit.log does not exist because no FORCE/reset events occurred during the run. Per runbook this is the expected condition.

tests.name: no_orphaned_tempfiles
tests.details: find .claude -name '*.tmp' returns nothing.

summary: Private POC desktop standard agent confirms agent-1's findings (init.sh push failure, no phase-advance script, BL-006 shell bypass) and adds two new minor gaps (lint-uat-scenarios usage undocumented; runbook Section E silent on POC Phase 4 block). POC mode behavior is correct end-to-end.
